description: Inspect tsconfig, package exports, emitted files, and import sites to validate module resolution and ESM/CJS boundaries.
body: Inputs: failing import, package.json, tsconfig, source path, emitted output when available, and consumer path. Procedure: compare module/moduleResolution, package type, exports/types fields, source extension, emitted extension, and whether callers use import or require. Check whether path aliases exist only in TypeScript or are also supported by runtime/bundler. Do not add ambient declarations or alias rewrites until the runtime path is understood. Output: module-contract diagnosis with type-level status, runtime-loader risk, and validation command. Verification signals include package-local typecheck, runtime smoke, and consumer import success. Use when TypeScript / JavaScript work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: run the documented inspection and validation workflow; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
